title: Help Desk Support II
description: POSITION TITLE: Help Desk Technician II LOCATION: North Bay, ON Salary: $68,490 – $75,204 CAD Annually About the Position: The Help Desk Technician II is responsible in assisting MacLean Engineering employees at all locations with all help desk related activities including hardware and software support. Duties and Responsibilities: Provide second level support for all hardware and software issues. Prioritize, respond to, and resolve escalated technical support requests in a timely manner. Setup and configure desktop computers, laptops, mobile devices, tablets, monitors, printers, and peripheral devices as required. Install and support standard software installations. Maintain and update IT related assets and perform periodic hardware audits. Travel to other MacLean branches as required. Effectively prioritize tasks and manage multiple support requests simultaneously. Assist in network troubleshooting in collaboration with the network team. Apply logical problem solving and troubleshooting skills to diagnose and resolve issues. Document common issues and solutions to support knowledge sharing and continuous improvement. Escalate complex technical issues to senior IT staff as appropriate. Communicate technical information clearly and professionally to non-technical users. Demonstrate the ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment. Strong organizational and time management skills. Meticulous attention to detail with consistent follow through. Willingness to learn and adapt to new technologies and processes. Support IT projects and perform additional duties as assigned by the Business Systems Supervisor. Qualifications: Education and Experience A+ certification an asset. Post secondary diploma or certificate in Information Technology, Computer Systems, or a related field preferred. Previous help desk, technical support, or related IT experience preferred (3-5 years). Proficiency with Windows operating systems and Microsoft Office applications. Solid understanding of networking concepts. Experience with Active Directory, Linux, Office 365, MDM solutions, SolidWorks, DraftSight, and Dynamics 365 an asset. Strong commitment to confidentiality and data security practices. Bilingual fluency an asset. Working Conditions Office and Factory setting. Fast-paced, multitasking work environment. Required to wear PPE on shop floor. Onsite role with standard 8-hour shifts. Planned Training HR Orientation HSE Orientation – Fire Suppression System Vivantio ticketing and asset system Bona fide Occupational Requirements Must complete all required training Must possess a valid driver's license and clean driver’s abstract About Us: Founded in 1973 in Ontario, Canada, MacLean Engineering is the world’s largest Canadian-based manufacturer of underground mining equipment. While continuing to expand both our product range and global footprint, we remain privately owned and committed to the founder’s original design principles: safety, productivity, and enduring quality. Our company delivers mobile equipment solutions for both underground and surface mining, supported by unit-of-one manufacturing expertise and a global service network. Driven by a strong commitment to safety, productivity, and customer support, MacLean serves mining operations worldwide through branches in Canada, the United States, Mexico, Peru, South Africa, and Australia. We operate five manufacturing facilities located in Collingwood, Owen Sound, Barrie, North Bay, and Mexico, and employ over 1,000 people across six countries and four continents. The next chapters of MacLean’s mining innovation story are being written within the broad themes of electrification, automation, and digitalization of the global mining industry. Our skilled tradespeople work in a safety‑first environment to build high‑quality components and equipment for the underground mining, municipal, environmental, and industrial sectors. Join MacLean and contribute to a legacy defined by innovation, craftsmanship, and meaningful impact. We offer competitive wages and benefits including: 3 weeks' vacation annually Paid Overtime after 40 hours worked Flexible Group benefits Package (Once probationary period is completed) Voluntary DPSP (Deferred Profit-Sharing Plan) and GRRSP (Group Registered Retirement Savings Plan) (Once probationary period is completed) Annual Employee Bonus $250.00 Annual Safety Shoe allowance Please note that employment with MacLean Engineering is contingent upon the successful completion of a background check conducted by our third-party provider, Sterling Backcheck. This process may include verification of employment history, education, and a criminal record check, depending on the requirements of the position. A clear and satisfactory result is required for all candidates prior to the confirmation of employment. MacLean Engineering & Marketing Co. Limited is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to providing employment accommodation in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (2005). MacLean Engineering & Marketing Co. Limited will provide accommodations to job applicants with disabilities throughout the recruitment process. If you require an accommodation, please notify us and we will work with you to meet your needs.

title: Drillers
description: Work with one of the world’s leading providers of specialized drilling services supporting exploration projects across the globe Earn competitive compensation based on experience and contract assignment, along with comprehensive benefits, RRSP matching, paid travel, and accommodation while on rotation Join a global team with opportunities to grow your career while contributing to exploration projects that support the future of the mining industry About Major Drilling Major Drilling Group International Inc. is the world’s leading provider of specialized drilling services for the metals and mining industry, supporting exploration and development programs in some of the most demanding environments on the planet. Established in 1980, the company has grown into a recognized global brand trusted by mining companies to deliver precision drilling on complex geological challenges. With field operations and registered offices across North America, South America, Australia, Asia, Africa, and Europe, Major Drilling works alongside clients ranging from junior explorers to major producers. The company’s reputation is built on the expertise of a highly skilled workforce, strong operational leadership, and regional teams known for delivering consistent, high-quality project management. Employees join a team recognized for its strong safety culture, technical expertise, and commitment to training and development, contributing directly to the discovery and advancement of mineral resources that support modern life. For more information, please visit our website: majordrilling.com About the Opportunity Major Drilling Group International Inc. is looking for full-time Drillers based across Canada. Reporting to the Foreman, you will play a pivotal role in operating and maintaining drill equipment on surface diamond drilling projects, supervising a Driller's Assistant, and supporting safe, productive drilling programs in remote locations across the country. In this position, you will be primarily responsible for completing drilling activities efficiently and safely, maintaining equipment performance, supporting daily site operations, and representing Major Drilling professionally on client sites and in camp environments. More specifically, your responsibilities include but are not limited to: Operating surface diamond drills safely and efficiently in varying ground conditions and remote environments Performing routine maintenance, troubleshooting, and minor repairs to keep equipment running reliably Overseeing and directing the work of the Driller's Assistant on the drill Monitoring drill performance, production targets, and daily site requirements Handling drill setup, dismantling, and reassembly for skid-based and heli-portable projects as required Completing daily checks, documentation, and housekeeping in line with company and site expectations Following HSEC policies, safe work procedures, and site-specific requirements at all times Representing the company professionally with clients, camp staff, and site personnel To read the full position description, please click here. Work is conducted on rotational schedules depending on the contract and location, commonly including 28 days on/14 days off, 14 days on/14 days off, or 14 days on/7 days off. About You To qualify, you will need previous drilling experience within mineral exploration or mining operations. Ideally, you will bring 1–2 years of diamond drilling experience; however, a combination of relevant drilling experience, training, and the right attitude will also be considered. The following are required: Ability to pass pre-employment drug and alcohol testing Ability to meet site medical and fitness requirements Valid First Aid Level C with CPR, or the willingness to obtain it Willingness and ability to travel and work rotational schedules at remote project sites across Canada, with location preferences considered where possible Additionally, the following skills and background will be highly valued: Experience with surface diamond drilling operations Familiarity with heli-portable or skid-based drilling setups Mechanical aptitude for maintaining and troubleshooting drill equipment Experience working in remote exploration or camp environments Ability to supervise and work effectively with a Driller’s Assistant Knowledge of safe drilling practices and site safety expectations Experience working with varying ground conditions and drilling environments Common Core training or other relevant industry certifications (asset) As our ideal candidate, you will possess a strong safety mindset, a dependable work ethic, and the ability to work independently in remote environments. Your professionalism, reliability, and practical problem-solving ability will help you manage daily drilling operations while maintaining production expectations and equipment performance. This is a hands-on field position suited to someone who takes pride in their work and is ready to contribute to challenging drilling programs across Canada, joining a team that values skill, accountability, and a commitment to safe, high-quality drilling operations. About the Benefits Compensation varies based on skills, experience, and contract location. Competitive hourly wages, ranging from $32 to $45 per hour, plus an industry leading bonus structure (contract dependent), and a host of excellent benefits including: RRSP matching program to support long-term financial planning Comprehensive health and dental benefits, including short-term and long-term disability coverage Accommodation provided while working remotely Company-provided PPE and safety equipment for all job sites Pre-employment and site-specific training provided where required Reliable equipment, industry leading safety, opportunities for growth Opportunities to gain experience across a variety of drilling environments and advance into more senior positions within the organization Ready to dig into your next opportunity? Join Major Drilling and apply today!

title: Solutions Architect - Epicor ERP - Canada
description: Solution Architect (Epicor ERP) Department: Development Team Company Overview Six S Partners is a recognized leader in Epicor ERP implementations, customizations, and management consulting. As Epicor’s “Global Partner of the Year” multiple years and a “Certified Platinum Partner,” we deliver tailored ERP solutions to mid-market companies worldwide. Role Summary The Solution Architect is part of Six S Partners’ Development Team and acts as the key link between our customers and our developers. You will lead discovery and scoping conversations, determine whether requirements can be met through Epicor’s standard functionality or require customization, and translate outcomes into clear design documents and user stories. You’ll also own validation (testing delivered work against the design) then produce the customer-facing documentation that explains what was built and how to use it. If you love Epicor, enjoy solving complex problems, working directly with customers, and take pride in reading and writing excellent documentation, this role will fit you well. Key Outcomes •Customer needs are clearly defined, validated, and translated into build-ready requirements •The right approach is chosen (Epicor standard vs customization) and documented •Developers have unambiguous user stories and acceptance criteria to build from •Delivered work is thoroughly tested and meets expectations before release •Customers receive clear documentation that explains the delivered functionality •Agile delivery stays organized, predictable, and transparent Responsibilities •Lead discovery/scoping calls to understand requirements, constraints, and success criteria •Evaluate Epicor’s out-of-the-box capabilities vs customization; recommend the best path forward •Write high-quality design documents and user stories that customers and developers can both understand •Define acceptance criteria and ensure requirements are testable and complete •Own backlog creation and refinement, sprint planning, and coordination of sprint ceremonies (review, retro, etc.) •Test delivered functionality against the design; provide clear feedback to developers to close gaps •Produce customer-facing documentation (how it works, how to use it, what changed) •Partner closely with developers and internal stakeholders to remove ambiguity and keep delivery moving Skills and Qualifications Must have: •Strong Epicor ERP knowledge, especially operations and/or finance modules (5 years experience) •Hands-on experience with Epicor tools (BAQs, BPMs, Functions, AppStudio) •Excellent writing skills and a genuine interest in reading/writing documentation daily •Strong analytical mindset, able to turn messy requirements into clear, testable user stories •Comfortable leading customer conversations, asking sharp questions, and clarifying scope •Experience running agile delivery (backlog, refinement, sprint planning, story writing) •Strong organizational skills in situations that are fast paced with high workloads. Nice to have •SSRS reporting experience and related Microsoft technologies •C# development experience •Jira experience Why This Role You’ll have real ownership: you help shape what gets built, you validate that it works, and you document it so customers succeed. You’ll work in a collaborative dev environment where good requirements and good documentation are treated as core to quality delivery. Job Type: Full-time Pay: $80,000.00-$90,000.00 per year Experience: Epicor ERP: 5 years (required) Work Location: Remote

title: Senior Engineer, Hardware ($120,000+/yr and Relocation Assistance to Aurora, ON)
description: Drive innovation in defence and aerospace with WOLF’s cutting-edge technology Earn a competitive annual salary of $120,000-$150,000 with comprehensive benefits Join WOLF’s inclusive and supportive team to shape the future of mission-critical solutions worldwide About Wolf Advanced Technology At Wolf Advanced Technology (WOLF), we lead the world in designing and developing rugged, field-deployable solutions that capture, process, encode, and display embedded video for the most demanding military and aerospace markets. Founded with a commitment to innovation, our advanced products meet the rigorous standards necessary for mission-critical success, serving top defence contractors and aerospace leaders globally. Our key technologies include NVIDIA, Xilinx, and AMD processing units, which power our cutting-edge solutions for AI machine vision, neural network-based deep learning, and more. Our high-energy, results-driven team thrives in an environment of rapid business growth and continuous innovation. At WOLF, we cherish a culture of collaboration where every voice is valued. If you excel in fast-paced development and are excited by leading-edge technology, WOLF is the company for you. Join us and contribute to groundbreaking, lifesaving, mission-critical systems. For more information, please visit our website: wolfadvancedtechnology.com Your Mission Brief WOLF Advanced Technology is on the hunt for a full-time Sr. Engineer, Hardware to join our innovative, mission-focused engineering team in Aurora, ON. Reporting to the VP, Engineering, you’ll play a mission-critical role in leading the design and development of complex mixed-signal hardware systems that push the limits of defense and aerospace technology. In this hybrid role, you’ll work closely with cross-functional experts in software, firmware, and PCB design, driving projects from concept to successful integration. This is a hardware-focused position where hands-on problem solving, deep technical expertise, and rapid iteration are part of the everyday rhythm. As Sr. Engineer, Hardware at WOLF, you might… Lead high-impact hardware design: Own the development of cutting-edge mixed-signal systems from requirements through to validated product Engineer with precision: Create complete hardware designs, sub-systems, and functional specifications that align with demanding product goals Select the right tech for the mission: Conduct feasibility studies and choose components that balance performance, cost, and operational needs Bring designs to life: Implement circuit schematics, lead bring-up, and troubleshoot using oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, and other advanced tools Collaborate across disciplines: Partner with software, firmware, and PCB engineers to ensure seamless product integration Ensure mission readiness: Maintain compliance with rigorous industry standards and document designs for full traceability Mentor and inspire: Guide junior engineers and foster a culture of technical excellence Innovate for performance: Identify opportunities to improve design performance through data-driven testing and analysis This is your opportunity to shape mission-critical solutions that operate where others fail. At WOLF, your expertise will directly influence the success of defense and aerospace programs worldwide. To read the full position description, please click here. Your Edge You hold a Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering or a related field (or equivalent experience), with 10+ years of hands-on mixed-signal hardware design and development experience. You’ve engineered systems featuring high-speed interfaces like PCIe Gen4, DDR4, and GDDR6, integrated video standards such as 3G-SDI, 12G-SDI, DisplayPort 1.4, and HDMI 2.0, and are fluent in using advanced lab equipment including oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, and precision measurement tools. Your engineering expertise is matched by your discipline in planning and documentation. You create complete hardware functional specifications, translate requirements into robust designs, and maintain full traceability for compliance and quality. You know how to troubleshoot at the circuit level, perform detailed signal integrity analysis, and optimize designs for both performance and manufacturability. You work seamlessly across disciplines, collaborating with software, firmware, and PCB engineers to ensure flawless integration. Whether you’re mentoring junior team members, managing third-party design partners, or pushing the boundaries of design performance, you take ownership from concept to deployment. You’re also well-versed in operating within CGP and ITAR frameworks, understanding the rigor and precision required in defense and aerospace projects. If you excel in high-tech, high-pressure environments where innovation meets uncompromising quality, you’ll thrive as part of WOLF’s mission-driven team. Wolfpack Perks At WOLF, we invest in you – not just through a competitive base tailored to your skills and experience, but with a benefits package designed to support your health, growth, and work-life balance. Here’s what you can look forward to: Top-Tier Compensation: $120,000-$150,000 CAD (negotiable based on your expertise) Wellness & Coverage: Health Care Spending Account, short- and long-term disability plans, and a monthly Wellness Allowance Generous Time Off: Three weeks’ paid vacation plus an additional week of company-wide holiday shutdown Flexible Work Model: Balance in-office collaboration with time at home—whatever helps you perform at your best Profit Sharing: Participate in our profit-sharing program after one year of service Relocation Support: Assistance available for the right candidate to make your move seamless Professional Development: Budget for training, conferences, and memberships to keep your skills ahead of the curve Team Culture: Regular social events—from food-truck lunches and sports outings to bi-weekly wellness mornings and holiday parties Join us at WOLF and be part of a team dedicated to delivering mission-critical solutions for the world's most demanding markets. Apply today! Ready to Unlock Your Mission? At WOLF, we thrive on innovation—but we also play by strict defence rules. To join our pack, applicants must be able to provide valid documentation to show Canadian or US citizenship or Canadian Permanent Residency and undergo a police check with no criminal background. This posting is for an existing vacancy. We use automated tools, including artificial intelligence, to support parts of our recruitment and selection process. These tools may be used to screen or assess applications.

Position Overview: Solutions Sales Professional The Solution Sales Professional candidate will be dedicated to driving skilled Microsoft Consulting pre-sales enterprise-level solutions with a focus on customer satisfaction, project timelines, and deliverables. This role will have the required senior-level pre-sales experience and their primary focus will be on pre-sales activity. Collaborate closely with account teams, technology partners, and Microsoft to ensure alignment with best practices and current programs. Represent Compugen professionally in customer meetings, presentations, and business events, delivering with confidence and impact. Skills & Qualifications: Bilingualism required (French and English), oral and written. title: Director Of Consulting Practice ( Financial Services | Salesforce | AI)
description: Company Overview Navirum is a consulting firm specializing in digital transformation for financial services organizations across North America. We work with wealth managers, asset managers, banks, and financial institutions to modernize operations using Salesforce, cloud platforms, and emerging AI technologies. Our focus is helping firms improve operational efficiency, client experience, and technology integration across their businesses. As the financial services industry evolves, we are building a modern consulting practice that blends deep cloud consulting expertise with practical AI capabilities. Navirum is entering its next phase of growth. We are expanding our consulting practice by bringing in experienced consultants who want to deliver high-impact client work today and help build the consulting organization of tomorrow. SUMMARY We are seeking a Director of Consulting Practice (Financial Services | Salesforce | AI) to help scale Navirum’s consulting capabilities and contribute to the long-term growth of the firm. This role is designed as a player-coach consulting leadership role. Initially, the focus will be on leading consulting engagements and delivering client value. Over time, the role expands into building and scaling the consulting practice, including mentoring consultants, developing repeatable services, and contributing to the growth of the firm. THE ROLE The position blends: Client consulting (~80%) Leading discovery workshops Designing digital transformation solutions Guiding Salesforce implementations Advising financial services organizations on operational modernization Practice development (~20%) Developing consulting frameworks and delivery methods Supporting business development and pre-sales Contributing to thought leadership and industry engagement Helping grow the consulting team as the practice expands This role will work closely with company leadership, technical services, and operations to deliver successful client outcomes and grow Navirum’s consulting practice. Over time, this role is expected to evolve into leading a small consulting team and helping shape the future of Navirum’s consulting capabilities. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Client Consulting & Solution Leadership Lead discovery workshops and strategic consulting engagements with financial services clients. Design digital transformation solutions for wealth managers, asset managers, and financial institutions. Guide complex Salesforce implementations, particularly Salesforce Financial Services Cloud. Provide expertise on operational transformation within wealth and asset management organizations. Advise clients on integrating portfolio management systems and related financial services technology platforms. Act as a trusted advisor to clients on CRM strategy, data architecture, and digital transformation initiatives. Consulting Practice Development Help develop consulting frameworks, delivery playbooks, and methodologies that strengthen Navirum’s consulting practice. Build repeatable consulting services and packaged offerings. Contribute to the development of internal consulting knowledge and assets, including methodologies documented in The Navirum Way. Support the evolution of Navirum’s consulting model as the firm grows. Over time, contribute to building and leading a small consulting team. AI-Enabled Consulting Explore practical ways AI can improve consulting delivery and client outcomes. Help integrate AI tools into consulting workflows and internal processes. Contribute to the development of AI-enabled consulting services that complement Navirum’s Salesforce expertise. Maintain familiarity with emerging technologies such as Agentforce, AI agents, and modern LLM platforms. Business Development & Market Engagement Support pre-sales activities and solution design for new consulting opportunities. Participate in client workshops, industry events, and strategic conversations. Contribute to consulting thought leadership and content development. Help shape new consulting services aligned with evolving market demand. Team Leadership Mentor consultants and elevate consulting quality across the team. Help establish delivery standards and consulting best practices. Support hiring and development of additional consulting talent as the practice grows. Contribute to building a collaborative consulting culture focused on client success and continuous improvement. COMPENSATION Base salary (competitive) Performance bonus - Up to 25% Equity: Long-term equity incentive aligned with company's growth Additional Benefits: PTO Paid education leave Wellness days Volunteer days IDEAL BACKGROUND The ideal candidate combines strong consulting experience, financial services expertise, and a builder mindset. ( Financial Services | Salesforce | AI), and has a Bachelor degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or Information Technology. This role is particularly well suited for consultants who have developed their skills within boutique consulting firms and are looking to take the next step into practice leadership and consulting practice growth. EXPERIENCE REQUIRED 8–15 years of technology consulting or digital transformation experience within Financial Services. Strong experience working with wealth management or asset management firms in North America. Minimum 5 years of hands-on Salesforce experience (implementation, architecture, or advisory). Mandatory experience with Salesforce Financial Services Cloud. Familiarity with portfolio management systems and financial services technology platforms. Understanding of regulatory and compliance considerations within financial services. Experience delivering digital transformation initiatives within complex organizations. Exposure to emerging AI tools, LLM platforms, and AI-enabled consulting approaches. Experience contributing to the growth of a consulting practice or boutique consulting firm. TRAITS WE VALUE The right candidate will demonstrate: A practice builder mindset. Structured and methodical thinking. Strong consulting instincts and client engagement skills. Comfort working in fast-moving and evolving environments. Curiosity about how AI will reshape consulting work. A pattern-oriented approach to consulting and solution design. Warm, collaborative leadership combined with strong professional standards. Low ego, high accountability, and strong team orientation. Why This Role Matters This role will help shape the next phase of Navirum’s growth as we scale a consulting practice focused on Salesforce, financial services transformation, and AI-enabled consulting. You will play a key role in: delivering impactful consulting engagements developing consulting methodology mentoring consultants expanding the consulting practice For the right candidate, this role provides an opportunity to grow from leading engagements today to helping build and lead a consulting practice over time. Job Types: Full-time, Permanent Pay: $145,000.00-$155,000.00 per year Benefits: Paid time off Tuition reimbursement Wellness program Work from home Application question(s): Are you legally authorized to work in Canada without employer sponsorship? (Yes / No) What are your salary expectations for this role? Please indicate your desired salary range. How many years of experience you have with Salesforce Financial Services Cloud? How many years of experience you have working with wealth management or asset management firms in North America? How many years of experience you have in consulting /delivering digital transformations (Salesforce) ? Education: Bachelor's Degree (required) Experience: Digital Transformation (Salesforce): 8 years (required) Work Location: Remote

title: Plant Operator
description: SUMMARY Plant Operator is tasked to duties to install, troubleshoot, repair and maintain facility equipment and building according to safe, predictive and productive maintenance systems and processes to support the achievement of the site’s business goals and objectives. Given the risk associated with mission critical facilities, adherence to established processes and risk management practices is important in order to ensure the reliability of operations and achievement of facility uptime objectives. The Plant Operator is responsible for performing work in accordance with processes and procedures complying with internal and external requirements including but not limited to environmental, health and safety, fire protection and risk management. KEY DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES Operates plant equipment and building systems on rotating shifts, nights and or days; observes instruments, gauges and computerized operating data; starts up, shuts down and adjusts controls on equipment manually and by computer; complies with regulations and standards; inspects operating conditions and reports malfunctions; responds to alarms in operating systems; performs rounds and verifies operating equipment; contribute information regarding procurement of parts and plant supplies. Updates a shift logs; observes and records instrument, gauge and meter readings; performs calculations related to water, gas and electricity consumption; prepares reports for management on operational issues or deficiencies. Investigates reports and takes action on conditions that affect the safety and security of the plant and staff; prepares reports; performs housekeeping duties, provides a team work “safety watch” Performs work in accordance to established processes and risk management practices to ensure achievement of facility uptime objectives and to mitigate risks Complies with all internal and external requirements including but not limited to environmental, health and safety, fire protection and risk management The work does not require substantive supervision but will include coaching and reviewing the work of new employees, and contractors performing work within the plant or systems Has custody of and is responsible for a range of heating plant equipment and systems. These include: a diesel electric generator, computer systems that control the plant or facility, instruments and gauges, controls to regulate plant output, the plant distribution system, oil and gas fired high pressure boilers, combustion chambers, firing equipment, water pumps, valves, expansion joints, fans, hand and power tools and water samples. The work includes responsibility for a shift log that details plant operating conditions and matters that may affect plant safety and security. Is required make tours of inspection, record observations and to ensure that readings on instruments and gauges comply with regulations, operating procedures and occupational health and safety standards. Interprets information for colleagues, clients and supervisors on operational issues, deficiencies or changes in normal operations and new equipment; relays verbal instruction, as received from supervisors or colleagues regarding inspection and repair. Troubleshoots to identify mechanical equipment problems. Creativity is required to implement solutions to mechanical breakdowns. The actions performed prevent damage to the plant and equipment, increased operating costs and the interruption of service to clients. 25 % Other duties as assigned, including Mechanic Generalist. KNOWLEDGE & SKILLS High school diploma plus trades training, certificate of qualification and or licensed in one or more of the involuntary trades. 3-5 years of facility operations and maintenance work experience, as a Technician/Operator, ideally with at least 3 years of critical facility operations and maintenance work experience in environments such as heating cooling plant environment. Effort is needed to understand and apply established work procedures and prescribed operating methods. Guidance is available in manuals and standard operating procedures. Must understand and follow manufacturer directions and interpret mechanical drawings. Intellectual effort is required to investigate conditions that may affect the safety and security of the plant and persons in it. Unusual situations are referred to the supervisor. Must have significant knowledge of the operation of heating and cooling plant equipment and systems. This includes a familiarity with the operating characteristics of boilers, chillers, generators, combustion chambers, firing equipment, water pumps, valves, expansion joints, fans, distribution systems and auxiliary equipment including computer systems. This knowledge is needed to deliver to clients a safe and continuous control of temperature to achieve their specific environmental requirements. The work requires knowledge of testing methods to carry out tests on mechanical and electrically operated equipment and water samples. This knowledge is necessary to assure the reliability of plant equipment and to determine and adjust chemical concentrations in water. The work requires general knowledge of federal and provincial regulations related to environmental standards and occupational health and safety in plant operations. Strong ability to perform work in a safe and effective manner. Trade Certification of Electrician, Plumber, HVAC, Millwright or 4th class Engineer will be an asset WORKING CONDITIONS Must be able and willing to work 12 hour rotating shifts, be available for shift replacement and emergency call-outs as they arise. Must be willing to wear personal protective equipment. Must meet security clearance requirements

title: Scheduler
description: MetricAid is an multi-national, physician scheduling business in North Bay, ON. We are agile, customer-centric, challenging, and our goal is to support an environment where medicine, wellness, and caring come first. Our work environment includes: Modern office setting Growth opportunities Casual work attire Lively atmosphere Safe work environment Hi there. You just stumbled across an amazing opportunity. This might just be one of the most interesting postings you see today! We are hiring a scheduler. You may be wondering what that means - trust us, everyone wonders the same thing. A scheduler collects data - like schedule rules, and the personal preferences and availability of the users, and then builds schedules for their clients. Essentially, we play a bit of human Sudoku to try and build amazing schedules for our clients using a custom software built in-house. As a scheduler you will be covering some evening shifts and weekend on calls (answering a support phone). These are split up among the team, and usually work out to be 1 evening a week, and a weekend every 1.5 months. Make sure to include a cover letter to tell us a bit about yourself, and why you think you’d be an asset to our team! Only applicants with cover letters will be considered - please use the word pounce in your cover letter to show us you took the time to read the posting. Responsibilities: Interfacing with clients - calls, emails and video conferences included Building schedules based on client rules, user preferences, and system data General admin/ office duties - we never say “that's not my job"! General client and team support Weekend and evening shifts required Answering client support tickets/calls Skill Set: Ability to adhere to timelines and meet set deadlines Attention to detail Ability to prioritize Critical thinking Ability to stay cool under pressure Enjoy working with clients and colleagues Great communication skills - conversational Italian is an asset! Conflict resolution skills Strong computer knowledge Qualifications: To be honest, no one goes to school or has exact experience for scheduling, as such we aren’t looking for an exact qualification. The following job experience would be an asset: Medical Administration, Scheduling, Client Services, Customer Management, General Administration Mandatory - Education: Completion of Post secondary (University or College) Culture: MetricAid is unlike anywhere you will ever work. Everyone who works here is dedicated and passionate about the work we do, and that is in large part due to the culture we have built. We are a team. A team that works together and supports each other - and the odd Friday has pizza and plays board games or a social activity. If you’re interested in learning more about our company culture, check out our careers page - we even have a video on what it’s like to work here - https://www.metricaid.com/careers We also take Client Support and scheduling seriously - The key thing to know is that the reasons MetricAid is chosen above other scheduling companies is that we are dedicated to our clients and work to provide excellent support. Hiring Process: We take our time in the hiring process - our goal is to get to know you, and for you to get to know us. This means we generally have 3 touch points: Phone Interview In Person Interview, and Skills Test (related to the position) Benefits Flexible time off Health benefits after six months of employment Being a part of an AMAZING Team Job Types: Full-time, Permanent Pay: $45,000.00-$50,000.00 per year Benefits: Casual dress Company events Dental care On-site parking Paid time off Vision care Language: English (required) Work Location: In person

title: Scheduler
description: MetricAid is an multi-national, physician scheduling business in North Bay, ON. We are agile, customer-centric, challenging, and our goal is to support an environment where medicine, wellness, and caring come first. Our work environment includes: Modern office setting Growth opportunities Casual work attire Lively atmosphere Safe work environment Hi there. You just stumbled across an amazing opportunity. This might just be one of the most interesting postings you see today! We are hiring a scheduler. You may be wondering what that means - trust us, everyone wonders the same thing. A scheduler collects data - like schedule rules, and the personal preferences and availability of the users, and then builds schedules for their clients. Essentially, we play a bit of human Sudoku to try and build amazing schedules for our clients using a custom software built in-house. As a scheduler you will be covering some evening shifts and weekend on calls (answering a support phone). These are split up among the team, and usually work out to be 1 evening a week, and a weekend every 1.5 months. Make sure to include a cover letter to tell us a bit about yourself, and why you think you’d be an asset to our team! Only applicants with cover letters will be considered - please use the word pounce in your cover letter to show us you took the time to read the posting. Responsibilities: Interfacing with clients - calls, emails and video conferences included Building schedules based on client rules, user preferences, and system data General admin/ office duties - we never say “that's not my job"! General client and team support Weekend and evening shifts required Answering client support tickets/calls Skill Set: Ability to adhere to timelines and meet set deadlines Attention to detail Ability to prioritize Critical thinking Ability to stay cool under pressure Enjoy working with clients and colleagues Great communication skills - conversational Italian is an asset! Conflict resolution skills Strong computer knowledge Qualifications: To be honest, no one goes to school or has exact experience for scheduling, as such we aren’t looking for an exact qualification. The following job experience would be an asset: Medical Administration, Scheduling, Client Services, Customer Management, General Administration Mandatory - Education: Completion of Post secondary (University or College) Culture: MetricAid is unlike anywhere you will ever work. Everyone who works here is dedicated and passionate about the work we do, and that is in large part due to the culture we have built. We are a team. A team that works together and supports each other - and the odd Friday has pizza and plays board games or a social activity. If you’re interested in learning more about our company culture, check out our careers page - we even have a video on what it’s like to work here - https://www.metricaid.com/careers We also take Client Support and scheduling seriously - The key thing to know is that the reasons MetricAid is chosen above other scheduling companies is that we are dedicated to our clients and work to provide excellent support. Hiring Process: We take our time in the hiring process - our goal is to get to know you, and for you to get to know us. This means we generally have 3 touch points: Phone Interview In Person Interview, and Skills Test (related to the position) Benefits Flexible time off Health benefits after six months of employment Being a part of an AMAZING Team Job Types: Full-time, Permanent Pay: $45,000.00-$50,000.00 per year Benefits: Casual dress Company events Dental care On-site parking Paid time off Vision care Language: English (required) Work Location: In person

title: Pharmacy Assistant {CUPE C-26-006}
description: Position: Pharmacy Assistant – Part Time Department: Pharmacy The Pharmacy Assistant supports pharmacy operations by collecting best possible medication history (BPMH), packaging medications and assisting in the supply of controlled substances throughout the organization. Working under the supervision of the Pharmacist or Pharmacy Technician, they adhere to established standards of Hospital Pharmacy practice. Optional Program Incentive Are you a Pharmacy Assistant working toward registration with the Ontario College of Pharmacists and excited about a future at North Bay Regional Health Centre? We’ve got a great opportunity to support your journey! Through our Pharmacy Assistant Tuition Reimbursement Program, eligible candidates can receive up to $5,000 in educational funding upon successful completion of the Pharmacy Technician Program and registration with the Ontario College of Pharmacists within two (2) years of hire. In return, participants commit to two years of employment at North Bay Regional Health Centre as a Regulated Pharmacy Technician. This program is completely optional and not a requirement of the position. QUALIFICATIONS Certification/Experience/Knowledge: High School diploma or equivalent Minimum of 5 years’ experience as a pharmacy assistant or equivalent Skills/Abilities: Demonstrated ability to perform duties independently, with a high degree of motivation and minimal supervision required Demonstrated written and verbal communications skill required Valid class G driver’s license required Excellent computer skills with proficiency in Microsoft Office software and patient information systems an asset Demonstrated excellent judgment and proven analytical skills required Demonstrated ability to attend work on a regular basis required Experience working with automated technology an asset Demonstrated commitment to ensure the safety of fellow workers, patients, volunteers, and visitors Must support and contribute to a culture of safety and prevention of adverse health events in this organization Bilingualism (Advanced level English/French) is required. All unilingual candidates are encouraged to apply and may be considered if the identified French requirement has been met or there are no successful bilingual candidates. A formal French test will be conducted to evaluate the proficiency level. North Bay Regional Health Centre is committed to providing accessible employment practices that are in compliance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA). If you require an accommodation for disability during any stage of the recruitment process, please indicate this in your application. NORTH BAY REGIONAL HEALTH CENTRE IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

title: Construction Project Manager
description: Job Overview We are seeking a highly organized, motived and experienced Construction Project Manager to oversee and coordinate all aspects of construction projects from inception to completion. The ideal candidate will possess strong leadership skills, technical expertise in construction management, and proficiency with industry-standard software. This role offers an opportunity to lead diverse projects, ensure timely delivery, and maintain high-quality standards across all phases of construction. Key Responsibilities: Project Planning & Management: Develop and review project plans, including timelines, budgets, resource allocation, and risk management strategies. Oversee all phases of the construction project including planning, design, procurement, execution, and closeout ensuring adherence to plans, specifications, and quality standards. Monitor project progress, identify potential issues, and implement corrective actions as needed. Work with Estimators to prepare and submit project proposals, bids, and contracts. Team Leadership & Coordination: Lead, mentor, and motivate project teams, including superintendents, foremen, onsite personnel and subtrades. Coordinate with clients, architects, engineers, subcontractors, and suppliers to ensure seamless project execution. Assign project tasks, managing timelines and foster a collaborative and productive work environment. Budget & Cost Control: Manage project budgets, track expenses, and ensure cost-effective resource utilization. Approve invoices and manage payment schedules. Identify and implement cost-saving measures without compromising quality or safety. Quality & Safety Assurance: Ensure all construction activities comply with relevant building codes, regulations, and safety standards. Implement and enforce strict quality control measures throughout the project lifecycle. Conduct regular site inspections to monitor progress, quality, and safety. Client & Stakeholder Communication: Serve as the primary point of contact for clients, providing regular updates on project status, progress, and any potential issues. Manage client expectations and ensure their satisfaction with project outcomes. Prepare and present project reports to stakeholders. Risk Management: Identify potential project risks and develop mitigation strategies. Resolve any conflicts or issues that arise during the project. Qualifications: Education & Experience Bachelor's degree or College diploma in Construction Management, Engineering, Architecture, Technologist or a related field. (or equivalent experience). 3+ years of experience in construction project management. PMP certification or equivalent is a plus. Skills and Competencies That Set You Apart Proven track record of successfully delivering complex construction projects on time and within budget. Strong knowledge of construction methods, materials, and regulations. Proficiency in project management software (e.g. Microsoft Project, BlueBeam, Procore or similar). Experience with construction accounting and cost control systems. Excellent leadership, organizational, communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills. Ability to read and interpret blueprints, schematics, and construction documents. Valid driver's license and ability to travel to various job sites. Working Conditions Split time between office and construction sites. Occasional evening and weekend work required. Local travel to project sites required. Compensation and Benefits: Competitive salary based on experience. Comprehensive health and dental benefits. Professional growth and training program. Company vehicle, cell phone and laptop. Performance-based bonuses. SPECIAL NOTE: Due to immediate hiring needs, applicants must be legally authorized to work in Canada at the time of application. Only candidates who are currently authorized to work in Canada without the need for employer sponsorship will be considered. Pay: $70,000.00-$150,000.00 per year Benefits: Dental care Extended health care Paid time off Profit sharing RRSP match Work Location: In person

title: Building Manager
description: Job Summary We are seeking an experienced and proactive Building Manager to oversee the daily operations, maintenance, and safety of our facility. The ideal candidate will possess strong management skills, technical expertise in industrial maintenance, and leadership abilities to ensure the building functions efficiently and safely. This role offers an opportunity to lead a team, manage projects, and maintain a high standard of operational excellence within a manufacturing or industrial environment. Duties Supervise and coordinate maintenance activities, including mechanical, electrical, HVAC, and low voltage systems. Oversee the implementation and management of CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System) for tracking work orders and maintenance schedules. Manage projects related to building upgrades, repairs, and safety improvements using project management best practices. Lead and supervise maintenance staff and contractors, ensuring adherence to safety protocols and quality standards. Read and interpret schematics, wiring diagrams, and technical manuals related to industrial equipment and systems. Ensure compliance with safety regulations and conduct regular safety inspections. Oversee the installation, troubleshooting, and repair of programmable logic controllers (PLCs) and other automation systems. Coordinate with external vendors for specialized services such as HVAC servicing or electrical work involving low voltage systems. Maintain accurate documentation of maintenance activities, inspections, and repairs using CMMS tools. Develop preventive maintenance schedules to minimize downtime and optimize operational efficiency. Experience Proven management or supervisory experience in an industrial or manufacturing setting. Strong background in mechanical knowledge, electrical systems, HVAC systems, and low voltage wiring. Experience with project management methodologies for facility upgrades or repairs. Familiarity with programmable logic controllers (PLCs), schematics, and technical diagrams. Knowledge of CMMS software for maintenance tracking is highly desirable. Excellent leadership skills with the ability to supervise teams effectively. Strong communication skills in English for clear reporting and coordination. Prior experience in industrial maintenance or facilities management is preferred. This position requires a dedicated professional capable of managing complex building systems while leading a team towards operational excellence in a dynamic environment. Job Type: Full-time Pay: From $23.00 per hour Expected hours: 40 per week Work Location: In person
